description:
Enpass is a cross-platform password management app to securely store passwords and other credentials in a virtual vault locked with a master password. Unlike most other popular password managers, Enpass is an offline password manager.


This release brings smoother navigation, and several reliability, display, and localization fixes.
- Activation works reliably after reconnecting to the internet
  The activation process now recovers smoothly when your internet connection is restored after an initial network error. Previously, an SSL error could persist even after connectivity was back, requiring a restart. You can now simply retry and continue without restarting Enpass.
- Notes field now stays expanded reliably
  The Notes field now stays expanded as expected. Once you expand the Notes field for any item, it remains expanded across all items until you manually collapse it or restart the app. Previously, the Notes field could collapse unexpectedly.
- Notes text is now readable in Dark theme
  Text entered in the Notes field now displays in the correct color when using the Dark theme. Previously, the text could appear in black on a dark background, making it hard to read.
- Security score now displays correctly on launch
  The security score on the Home dashboard now shows the correct value as soon as the app launches. Previously, it could display an incorrect score until you navigated away and returned.
- Item editing options are now translated
  The item editing options (such as Attachment, Section, and Add Field) now display in your chosen language. Previously, these options appeared in English even when using a different language like Portuguese.
- Keyboard shortcut now copies the right password
  The keyboard shortcut for copying passwords in the Enpass Assistant now correctly copies the password of the item shown in search results. Previously, it could copy the password of a previously selected item instead.
- Importing from Sticky Password now preserves special characters correctly
  Importing data from Sticky Password now correctly handles umlauts and other non-ASCII characters (such as ä, ö, ü, ß, and é). Previously, these characters could appear garbled after import. Your imported data now displays exactly as it should.
- Copy passwords directly from Enpass Assistant
  You can now copy a password directly from the Enpass Assistant's password generator. This option was removed in a previous update, is now restored.
- Minor bug fixes and improvements
For Business Users
- Quickly access your vault storage location
  Business users can now open the storage location of their shared vaults directly from the Sharing Center, available for OneDrive, Teams, and SharePoint Sites vaults..
